title: Project Overview & Structure
description: Start with the README to understand the pbtracker project goals: a computer vision pipeline that tracks players and the ball in pickleball match videos. It explains the directory layout, quick start setup, and 5 sequential pipeline steps.

title: Project Settings and Environment Setup
description: Look at pyproject.toml and .python-version to understand the project configuration and target environment (Python 3.12). It defines critical dependencies such as boxmot (BoT-SORT tracker), lapx, opencv-python, streamlit, and ultralytics (YOLOv8).

title: Pipeline Orchestrator Entry Point
description: The main.py file acts as the primary execution engine. It defines the main() orchestration loop which initializes tracking models, coordinates frame iteration, loads/saves detection caches (stubs), runs physics and officiating analysis, and renders the annotated video output.

title: Interactive Court Boundary Selection
description: The manual_court_selector.py script provides an interactive OpenCV GUI tool. It runs on the first frame of a match video, requiring the user to click exactly 12 court boundary keypoints. These coordinates are cached to verify on-court containment and project coordinates to physical space.

title: Multi-Player Detection and Tracking
description: The player_tracker.py module is responsible for loading the pre-trained YOLOv8x model and tracking players across video frames. It uses expand_polygon_asymmetric() to expand the court boundary polygon, verifying if players are actively on-court or spectators off-court.

title: Pickleball Trajectory Tracking & Interpolation
description: The ball_tracker.py module uses a fine-tuned YOLOv5 model to detect the pickleball. Since ball detection often flickers due to fast movement and occlusion, it uses interpolate_ball_positions() which applies Pandas linear interpolation and back-filling to estimate missing frames.

title: Top-Down Court Projection (Homography)
description: The mini_court.py module performs top-down 2D court mapping. It uses OpenCV to calculate a 3x3 homography matrix mapping perspective-distorted pixel points to physical ground-level coordinates in feet. This is crucial for accurate kinematics and bounce localization.

title: Ball Physics & Kinematics Engine
description: The ball_physics_analyzer.py module processes ball coordinates over time. It computes instantaneous velocity and acceleration components, using change points and temporal cooldown locks to automatically detect paddle hits and court floor bounces.

title: Officiating & Line Boundary Decisions
description: The officiating_engine.py module handles automated referee calls. It maps court bounces detected by the physics analyzer to the physical court boundary lines. It determines if a bounce is IN or OUT and overlays large colored banners on the video stream.

title: Kinematic Telemetry & HUD Overlays
description: The stats_telemetry_tracker.py module calculates locomotion statistics for players, including running speed and total distance covered. It also detects paddle hits based on player-to-ball proximity and draws the top HUD overlay showing stats for each player.

title: Spatio-Temporal & HSV Track Stitching
description: The tracklet_merger.py script acts as the first-level track healer. It resolves fragmented tracker IDs by analyzing spatial centroid distance, temporal gaps, and visual similarity of players (computed via OpenCV HSV color histogram correlation).

title: Deep Neural Re-ID Identity Healing
description: The deep_reid_healer.py module runs deep learning-based identity recovery. It extracts 2048-dimensional appearance embeddings from player crops using a PyTorch ResNet50 model, matching late-appearing fragment tracks to baseline master profiles via cosine similarity.

title: Interactive Performance Dashboard
description: The app.py script mounts a Streamlit dashboard. It loads the cached player locomotion metrics, physics events, and rally logs to render interactive movement heatmaps, speed over time plots, and game summaries using Plotly visualizations.

title: Project Methodology & Algorithm Analysis
description: End the tour with METHODS_AND_RETROSPECTIVE.md. It documents the mathematical and geometrical formulas behind court homography, coordinate mapping, physics thresholding, and details failed tracking approaches and future improvements.
